objective: Repair the governance sequencing break for the org governance compendium work, create the canonical issue-backed plan before implementation resumes, decide under review whether to adopt or rework the existing dirty draft, and produce a deterministic generated compendium only from an isolated worktree with validation and closeout evidence.
scope_boundary: Use issue #223 as the governing issue for org governance compendium repair and the corrective-action record for the implementation-first sequencing break.
Treat the dirty shared-main compendium diff as unapproved draft source material until this plan is reviewed.
Create the canonical structured JSON plan, PDCAR companion, and process finding before adopting or implementing compendium changes.
Run alternate-family review before marking implementation ready.
If implementation proceeds, use only the isolated issue worktree and commit no files from the dirty shared main checkout.
Coordinate with issue #224 as Phase 0 dependency and route the broader governance-surface planning-gate hardening into #226 unless completed here as a narrow corrective slice.
out_of_scope: Mutating the shared main checkout at /Users/bennibarger/Developer/HLDPRO/hldpro-governance.
Committing the dirty draft before alternate-family review and explicit adoption under this plan.
Implementing the full always-on orchestrator, governed-repo registry, launchd observer, packet queue, or model runtime from issue #223.
Replacing GitHub Issues as the canonical execution tracker.
Relaxing Society of Minds no-self-approval, tier, cross-family, fallback, or PII constraints.
research_summary: Issue #223 documents that compendium implementation began before the required governance planning package existed. The dirty shared main checkout contains a draft generator, generated compendium, Markdown-only PDCAR, sweep integration, and registry documentation updates. Issue #224 now treats #223 as the separate Phase 0 dependency for the always-on orchestrator. The correct repair is to preserve the process finding, create canonical structured planning artifacts, add #223 to the backlog mirror, obtain alternate-family review, and only then decide whether the existing draft satisfies the approved plan or needs rework. The broader mechanical control gap is that structured-plan enforcement is branch-name-sensitive; the fix belongs either as a narrow corrective slice here or, preferably, in #226 planning/scope gatekeeper.

sprints.name: Sprint 1 - Planning repair package
sprints.goal: Create the issue-backed canonical planning package before any compendium implementation resumes.
tasks: Add the governance path repair findings document.
Add the canonical structured JSON plan for issue #223.
Add the PDCAR companion for issue #223.
Add issue #223 to OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md as active Phase 0 repair work.
Validate the structured plan and cross-check it against issue #223 acceptance criteria.
acceptance_criteria: docs/plans/issue-223-structured-agent-cycle-plan.json exists and passes the structured plan validator.
docs/plans/issue-223-org-governance-compendium-pdcar.md exists and does not contradict the JSON plan.
docs/plans/2026-04-17-governance-path-repair-findings.md records the sequencing break and repair path.
OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md references open issue #223.
No compendium implementation files are adopted in this sprint.

sprints.name: Sprint 2 - Alternate-family review and adoption decision
sprints.goal: Get independent architecture/standards review and decide whether the dirty draft can be adopted, needs rework, or should be discarded.
tasks: Run Claude alternate-family review against the canonical plan, PDCAR, repair finding, issue #223, and dirty draft inventory.
Record required changes in raw/cross-review/ if the plan is approved or approved with changes.
Apply required planning changes before implementation resumes.
Classify the existing draft as adopt, rework, or discard, with rationale.
Keep any implementation handoff blocked if review rejects the plan.
acceptance_criteria: raw/cross-review/ contains a schema-valid artifact for issue #223 if review approves or approves with changes.
The structured plan alternate_model_review field records the actual review result.
The adoption decision names the exact dirty draft files and whether each is adopted, reworked, or discarded.
Implementation handoff remains blocked if the review verdict is rejected or unavailable.
The Sprint 2 to Sprint 3 implementation boundary has explicit operator approval recorded before dirty draft adoption begins.

sprints.name: Sprint 3 - Compendium generator and generated artifact
sprints.goal: Adopt or rework the compendium generator and generated markdown only after reviewed planning approval.
tasks: Review the dirty draft generator as source material, not as approved code.
Implement or rework scripts/overlord/build_org_governance_compendium.py in the isolated issue worktree.
Generate docs/ORG_GOVERNANCE_COMPENDIUM.md from canonical governed repos.
Ensure worktree clones and issue-specific local clones are excluded.
Run compile and check-mode validation.
acceptance_criteria: scripts/overlord/build_org_governance_compendium.py compiles under python3.
docs/ORG_GOVERNANCE_COMPENDIUM.md is generated deterministically.
python3 scripts/overlord/build_org_governance_compendium.py --check passes.
The compendium indexes all governed repos named in the repo registry and issue #223.
Generated output clearly states it is generated and should be refreshed by the generator.

sprints.name: Sprint 4 - Sweep and registry integration
sprints.goal: Wire the compendium into weekly governance knowledge refresh only if the generator is stable in local and CI layouts.
tasks: Update overlord-sweep workflow only after the generator supports CI checkout paths.
Update the sweep agent instructions to refresh the compendium.
Update feature, service, and data dictionary entries for the implemented compendium behavior.
Route any registry dependency that requires #225 into issue #225 instead of hardcoding around it.
Run relevant validation and prepare Stage 6 closeout.
acceptance_criteria: Weekly sweep integration works with the workflow's repos/ checkout layout.
agents/overlord-sweep.md describes the compendium refresh without contradicting read/write scope.
docs/FEATURE_REGISTRY.md, docs/SERVICE_REGISTRY.md, and docs/DATA_DICTIONARY.md describe only implemented behavior.
Any remaining duplicate governed-repo discovery risk is tracked in #225 or #226 before #223 closes.
Stage 6 closeout exists under raw/closeouts/ before #223 is closed.

specialist_reviews.focus: Identify the sequencing break and required repair artifacts before compendium implementation resumes.
specialist_reviews.summary: The compendium work touched cross-repo governance docs, weekly sweep behavior, generated org indexes, and registry surfaces, so issue-backed planning and alternate-family review are required before adoption.

specialist_reviews.focus: Prevent contamination from the dirty shared main checkout while still preserving useful draft source material.
specialist_reviews.summary: The dirty draft may be read as source material, but implementation must occur in an isolated issue worktree from origin/main. No files should be committed directly from the shared main checkout.

specialist_reviews.focus: Separate planning repair, review/adoption decision, compendium implementation, sweep integration, and validator hardening.
specialist_reviews.summary: The plan separates the corrective planning package from implementation and routes the broader planning-gate hardening into #226 unless a narrow #223 fix is explicitly reviewed.

alternate_model_review.summary: Claude Opus 4.6 returned APPROVED_WITH_CHANGES. Blocking changes were to make approval metadata consistent and add an explicit Sprint 2 to Sprint 3 operator approval boundary. The current operator instruction on 2026-04-17 to proceed, loop through, and use no further HITL is recorded as approval for issue #223 implementation in this isolated worktree only. Recommended changes: keep the broader planning-gate bypass fix in #226, make closeout evidence explicit, and preserve model identity traceability within schema constraints.
evidence: STANDARDS.md Society of Minds cross-review requirements
Issue #223 requires specialist and alternate-family review before implementation resumes

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: This branch may add the #223 planning repair package, record review artifacts, and proceed to adopt or rework the dirty compendium draft in this isolated worktree only. The shared main checkout remains read-only source material.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Adopt or rework the dirty draft files in the isolated issue worktree, then run generator, compile, check-mode, plan, cross-review, and relevant sweep validation before PR.

material_deviation_rules: Do not write to or clean up the dirty shared main checkout.
If Claude review rejects the plan, do not implement compendium changes; update the plan or close the draft as rejected.
If the generator cannot support both local and CI checkout layouts, do not wire it into weekly sweep.
If the validator bypass fix grows beyond a narrow #223 corrective control, move it to #226 before closing #223.
If any file outside the sprint file_paths list needs edits, update the plan and review disposition before continuing.
If generated compendium output cannot be reproduced by --check, do not mark #223 complete.
Do not close #223 without Stage 6 closeout evidence.

Operator approval for the Sprint 2 to Sprint 3 boundary is recorded by the 2026-04-17 instruction to proceed, loop through, and use no further HITL for this repair sequence; any future expansion outside #223 scope still requires plan update.
approved_by: Operator instruction 2026-04-17: proceed, loop through, no HITL
